mnemonics.title: SCAM for Aggregate Functions
mnemonics.content: S - Sum (total), C - Count (how many), A - Average (mean), M - Max/Min (highest/lowest). Remember: 'Students Can Always Memorize' this set of five functions.

mnemonics.title: Maverick Views Are Pre-Built
mnemonics.content: M - Materialized (stores actual data), V - Virtual/View (stores query only). Think: 'Maverick' = concrete/real vs Virtual = phantom/blueprint.

mnemonics.title: FAST Refresh
mnemonics.content: F - Fast (incremental), A - Always (complete), S - Special (forced), T - Never/Today. Materialized view refresh types: 'Frequent Actions Save Time' to remember when to refresh.

mnemonics.title: GIRA - Group Items Really Assemble
mnemonics.content: G - GROUP BY, I - Include, R - Required, A - Aggregates. Remember: 'Group Items Really Assemble' - when creating aggregation views, GROUP BY is essential to organize aggregated data.

acronyms.usage: Use when you need pre-computed, stored results of expensive aggregate queries for faster performance.
Use when you need a logical representation of data without storing it physically; query is executed each time.
Use to update the data in materialized views - can be on-demand (manual) or scheduled (automatic).
Use to refer to views that use aggregate functions like SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX with GROUP BY.
Basic operations in simple views; aggregation views go beyond SPJ by including GROUP BY and aggregate functions.

visualTips.visualization: Picture a Virtual View as a photograph (image only, no substance), while a Materialized View is a 3D printed object (actual physical copy you can touch). The photograph shows what something looks like; the 3D print gives you the actual thing.

Imagine a factory assembly line: GROUP BY acts like sorting stations that group similar items together, and aggregate functions (SUM, COUNT, etc.) are the counters at each station that give you totals for each group.
Think of a supermarket price tag (materialized view) - it needs periodic refreshing/updating to match the actual inventory (base tables). Without refresh, the tag shows outdated information.

Picture a scoreboard during a cricket match - it shows aggregated runs (SUM), wickets (COUNT), strike rate (AVG) without having to count every ball individually each time you look. The scoreboard is pre-computed like a materialized aggregation view.

analogies.analogy: Like a pre-written summary at the beginning of a long book chapter - instead of reading the entire chapter every time to understand the main points, you read the pre-computed summary. The summary (materialized view) stores the results so you don't have to recalculate (query the base tables) every time.

Like a restaurant menu that shows recipes - it tells you HOW to make each dish (the query definition) but doesn't actually cook and store the food. Every time you want to eat (query the view), the kitchen (DBMS) has to cook (execute the query) fresh.

Like a monthly bank statement - it doesn't show every single transaction, but uses COUNT (number of transactions), SUM (total credits/debits), AVG (average daily balance) to give you a summary view. An aggregation view does exactly this for database tables.

Like syncing your phone contacts with the cloud - materialized views need periodic synchronization (refresh) to keep the cached data (materialized view) in sync with the original source (base tables). Without sync, your phone shows old contacts.
